description:
To properly set the group owner of /var/log/syslog, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp adm /var/log/syslog
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/syslog</tt> file contains logs of error messages in
the system and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil_clause:
/var/log/syslog does not have a group owner of
adm
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /var/log/syslog, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/syslog
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: adm
title: Verify Group Who Owns /var/log/syslog File
